fields.description:
Close the silent-success gap on the NativeOutput structured path (parity with PromptedOutput) plus two proven output self-heal tweaks on rebar's already-strong structured.py ladder. Small, cohesive, fully offline-verified.

## Why
A truncated / refused turn never raises at the model layer — it comes back as a complete-looking but unusable response — so exception classification can't see it; it needs a stop-reason check on the RESULT. rebar's PromptedOutput path already does this (`structured.check_stop_reason`, runner.py:519). But the **NativeOutput path (runner.py:497-502) returns `run_result.output` DIRECTLY with NO stop-reason check** — a truncated/refused NativeOutput response is returned as a hollow verdict. Separately, `structured.py` already implements almost the whole self-heal ladder (tolerant parse, deterministic json-repair before reask, error-fed reask, fast-fail on truncation, bounded `OUTPUT_RETRIES`) — it needs two cheap, proven additions.

## What
- **NativeOutput stop-reason parity (the core fix):** add `structured.check_stop_reason(getattr(run_result.response, "finish_reason", None))` on the NativeOutput branch (runner.py:497-502) BEFORE returning. A `length`/`max_tokens`/`content_filter`/`refusal` finish_reason then raises `UnretryableOutputError` (⊂ `StructuredOutputError` ⊂ `LLMError`) — exactly as the PromptedOutput path already does — so the gate degrades to an unsigned INDETERMINATE via `gate_dispatch`, never a hollow PASS/BLOCK. This REUSES the existing raise-based degrade path; it adds NO new routing, NO coverage-field change, NO truncation-vs-parse conjunction (the check runs BEFORE any parse, so truncation is always caught as the exception — there is no "parses-but-truncated" case). NOTE Anthropic (rebar's provider) uses PromptedOutput today; NativeOutput is the OpenAI-strict-schema path, fixed for correctness/parity.
- **Self-heal tweak 1 — schema-guided json-repair:** `structured.py:161` currently calls `repair_json(cand, return_objects=True)` WITHOUT a `schema=` arg. Add `schema=model_cls` (json-repair 0.61.2 accepts a pydantic v2 model directly; default `schema_repair_mode='standard'`) for schema-guided deterministic repair BEFORE any LLM reask, wrapped in a `try/except` that falls back to today's schema-less `repair_json` if the schema-guided call raises (never regresses current behavior). Requires threading `model_cls` into the tolerant-parse helper. Bump the `pyproject.toml` pin `json-repair>=0.30` → `>=0.61` (0.61.2 already installed — a floor raise, not a runtime change) so `schema=` is guaranteed.
- **Self-heal tweak 2 — faulty prior output in the reask:** `runner.py:530`'s reask sends instructions + schema_hint + the validation error, but NOT the model's raw faulty reply. Add a bounded snippet `str(result.output)[:_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS]` (named constant = 2000, truncated with an ellipsis marker) so the model can diff its own mistake (the LangChain RetryWithErrorOutputParser / Instructor pattern).
- **Deferred (follow-on, NOT here):** the #5221 case — a refusal returned as NON-EMPTY content with a NORMAL finish_reason and only an out-of-band `provider_details` signal — is NOT detected here; its exact Anthropic `provider_details` shape can't be characterized without a live refusal, so it is deferred IN FULL to a `discovered_from` follow-on ticket. This story adds NO speculative provider_details parsing. (Anthropic refusals today normalize to the finish_reason the parity fix already catches.)

## Scope
- The NativeOutput stop-reason parity fix + the two `structured.py`/`runner.py` self-heal tweaks + the pin bump. Cohesion: all are small, same-theme changes to the same structured-output reliability seam (`_pai_structured` + `structured.py`), share tests, and none is independently land-worthy — kept together to minimize integration overhead.
- NOT in scope: a second-interpreter "fixer" LLM (`structured.py` rejects it — keep that stance); verdict-schema edits (blackbear owns those); the deferred #5221 refusal-as-content case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The NativeOutput path runs `check_stop_reason`, so a truncated/refused NativeOutput response raises `UnretryableOutputError` → INDETERMINATE (parity with PromptedOutput), not a hollow verdict — test `test_native_output_path_checks_stop_reason`. A zero-finding CLEAN NativeOutput/PromptedOutput review still passes untouched — test `test_zero_finding_clean_review_passes`.
- [ ] `repair_json` is called with `schema=model_cls`, with a `try/except` fallback to the schema-less call — tests `test_repair_json_receives_schema` and `test_repair_json_falls_back_when_schema_call_raises`.
- [ ] The targeted reask includes the bounded faulty prior output (`_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS`) — test `test_reask_prompt_includes_bounded_prior_output`.
- [ ] No second-LLM fixer; no verdict-schema files edited. The deferred #5221 follow-on is created during the work (not a close-gate criterion).
- [ ] `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/structured.py` (schema-guided repair; thread model_cls), `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (NativeOutput check_stop_reason; bounded faulty output in reask), `pyproject.toml` (json-repair pin), `tests/unit/test_silent_success.py` (new); read-only on `src/rebar/schemas/*`.

## Verified library behavior (experiment log — pydantic-ai 1.107.0, offline)
ACTUAL captured output (no billable call):
```
ModelResponse fields: [parts, usage, model_name, timestamp, kind, provider_name, provider_url,
                       provider_details, provider_response_id, finish_reason, run_id, ...]
  -> finish_reason present (truncation/refusal signal, read by structured.check_stop_reason)
  -> provider_details present (the STRUCTURED out-of-band filter signal drake keys on, NOT a regex)
rebar structured.check_stop_reason(stop_reason: str | None) -> None   (reused for the truncation conjunction)
```
json-repair schema-guided repair, VERIFIED offline:
```
json-repair 0.61.2: repair_json params = [json_str, return_objects, skip_json_loads, logging, json_fd,
    chunk_length, stream_stable, strict, schema, schema_repair_mode, json_dumps_args]  -> schema + schema_repair_mode present
```
The current pin `json-repair>=0.30` predates the `schema=` arg; this story bumps it to `>=0.61` (0.61.2 is
already installed, so the bump is a floor-raise, not a runtime change). So the refusal-as-content detection keys on the structured provider signal (finish_reason +
provider_details), never a regex over answer text; and the truncation conjunction reuses the existing
check_stop_reason. A detected silent-success is routed through story mamba's shared LLMOutcome
(resolution_class=CHANGE_INPUT + sanitized diagnostic) — one routing path, not a second. These are
pydantic-ai LIBRARY response fields, not rebar symbols (bug succinct-formable-kite: repo-absence !=
non-existence). RED baseline (arawana, committed) pins today: canned content_filter/length finish_reason
-> UnretryableOutputError; text mode does NOT read finish_reason (the silent-success gap drake closes).
